key_insight: TREK expands a student model’s support on hard reasoning problems by using verified teacher-generated solutions for short forward-KL exploration, then switches back to GRPO for refinement.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical training recipe for boosting hard-task reasoning and agent performance even when the teacher is a black box or only inference-time context is available.
method: Identify prompts where the student has very low pass rate.
Query a proposal source for verified candidate solutions and keep the top-r ranked by student likelihood.
Apply a short forward-KL distillation phase to pull those modes into support, then resume on-policy GRPO.
evidence_signal: On Qwen3-8B, TREK improves AIME 2025 from 36.9 to 40.3 and AIME 2024 from 47.9 to 51.1 (avg@16), and also boosts ALFWorld and ScienceWorld success rates.
watch_item: Performance depends on finding verified proposals and selecting useful hard prompts, so compute cost and proposal quality may limit gains in practice.
paper_title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine
